title: heliconia aurea
intro: Heliconia aurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Heliconiaceae, commonly known as the golden lobster claw due to its bright yellow and red waxy bracts that resemble claws. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Latin America, where its leaves and flowers are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Heliconia aurea include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the flowers in tinctures and capsules.
